description: Review flashcards on glands, lymphoid tissue, bone, and cartilage histology

cards.text: What are the three types of exocrine secretory mechanisms?
cards.answer: Merocrine (exocytosis, cell intact), apocrine (apical cytoplasm buds off), holocrine (entire cell disintegrates)/merocrine apocrine holocrine

tip.tip: Sebaceous glands are the classic holocrine example

cards.text: How do you distinguish serous from mucous acini on H&E?
cards.answer: Serous: basophilic (blue) granular cytoplasm, round central nucleus, narrow lumen. Mucous: pale foamy cytoplasm, flattened basal nucleus, wide lumen/serous is blue with central nucleus; mucous is pale with basal nucleus

tip.tip: Serous = enzyme-rich watery secretion; mucous = viscous mucin

cards.text: Which salivary gland is purely serous, which is predominantly mucous, and which is mixed?
cards.answer: Parotid = purely serous, sublingual = predominantly mucous, submandibular = mixed with serous demilunes/parotid serous sublingual mucous submandibular mixed

tip.tip: Giannuzzi demilunes are serous caps on mucous tubules

cards.text: What are the main compartments of a lymph node and their functions?
cards.answer: Cortex (B-cell follicles with germinal centres), paracortex (T-cell zone), medulla (medullary cords with plasma cells and sinuses)/cortex paracortex medulla

tip.tip: Paracortex enlarges in viral infections (T-cell response)

cards.text: What is the difference between white pulp and red pulp of the spleen?
cards.answer: White pulp: lymphoid tissue (PALS = T cells, follicles = B cells) around central arterioles. Red pulp: splenic cords and sinusoids that filter blood/white pulp is lymphoid tissue; red pulp filters blood

tip.tip: The spleen filters blood, not lymph

cards.text: What are Hassall corpuscles and where are they found?
cards.answer: Concentrically arranged epithelial reticular cells found in the thymic medulla; involved in T-cell tolerance/concentrically arranged keratinised epithelial cells in thymic medulla

tip.tip: Key identification feature of the thymus

cards.text: Name the three types of cartilage and one location for each.
cards.answer: Hyaline (trachea, articular surfaces), elastic (ear pinna, epiglottis), fibrocartilage (intervertebral disc, pubic symphysis)/hyaline elastic fibrocartilage

tip.tip: Hyaline is the most common type

cards.text: What is the difference between compact and spongy bone?
cards.answer: Compact bone: dense, organised into Haversian systems (osteons) with concentric lamellae. Spongy bone: network of trabeculae with marrow spaces/compact has osteons; spongy has trabeculae

cards.text: What are the four types of bone cells and their functions?
cards.answer: Osteoprogenitor (stem cells), osteoblasts (bone formation), osteocytes (maintenance, mechanosensing), osteoclasts (bone resorption)/osteoprogenitor osteoblast osteocyte osteoclast

tip.tip: Osteoclasts are multinucleated cells derived from monocytes
